heading: Interest on overpayments
label: 6611 Interest on overpayments
notes: Editorial Notes

L. 105–2061997—Subsec. (f)(2), (3). , added par. (2) and redesignated former par. (2) as (3). Former par. (3) redesignated (4).Pub. L. 105–34, § 1055(b)(1)Subsec. (f)(4)(B)(i)(I). , substituted “paragraph (1), (2), or (3)” for “paragraph (1) or (2)”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1055(b)(2)(A)Subsec. (f)(4)(B)(ii)(II). , added subcl. (II). Former subcl. (II) redesignated (III).Pub. L. 105–34, § 1055(b)(2)(B)Subsec. (f)(4)(B)(ii)(III). , (C), redesignated subcl. (II) as (III) and inserted “(as defined in paragraph (3)(B))” after “case of a credit carryback”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1055(b)(2)(B)Subsec. (f)(4)(C)(i). , substituted “paragraph (1), (2), or (3)” for “paragraph (1) or (2)”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1055(b)(2)(A)Subsecs. (g) to (i). , redesignated subsecs. (h) and (i) as (g) and (h), respectively, and struck out former subsec. (g) which read as follows:Pub. L. 105–34, § 1055(b)(2)(D)“(g) .—For purposes of subsection (a), if any overpayment of tax results from a carryback of tax paid or accrued to foreign countries or possessions of the United States, such overpayment shall be deemed not to have been paid or accrued prior to the filing date (as defined in subsection (f)(3)) for the taxable year under this subtitle in which such taxes were in fact paid or accrued.”Refund of Income Tax Caused by Carryback of Foreign Taxes1993—Subsec. (e). amended heading and text of subsec. (e) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “If any overpayment of tax imposed by subtitle A is refunded within 45 days after the last date prescribed for filing the return of such tax (determined without regard to any extension of time for filing the return) or, in case the return is filed after such last date, is refunded within 45 days after the date the return is filed, no interest shall be allowed under subsection (a) on such overpayment.”Pub. L. 103–661988—Subsecs. (h) to (j). redesignated subsecs. (i) and (j) as (h) and (i), respectively, and struck out former subsec. (h) which related to special rule for windfall profit tax.Pub. L. 100–4181986—Subsec. (a). substituted “the overpayment rate established under section 6621” for “an annual rate established under section 6621”.Pub. L. 99–5141984—Subsec. (f)(3)(C). , added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 98–369, § 714(n)(2)(A)Subsec. (f)(4). , struck out par. (4) which provided that for purposes of subsection (a), if any overpayment of tax imposed by subtitle A arose by operation of section 815(d)(5) (relating to reduction of policyholders surplus account of life insurance companies for certain unused deductions), such overpayment would be deemed not to have been made prior to the close of the last taxable year to which the loss described in section 815(d)(5)(A) was carried under section 812(b)(2).Pub. L. 98–369, § 211(b)(27)1982—Subsec. (b)(3). , added par. (3).Pub. L. 97–248, § 346(a)Subsec. (f)(1). , substituted “the filing date for the taxable year” for “the close of the taxable year”.Pub. L. 97–248, § 346(c)(1)(A)Subsec. (f)(2)(A). , substituted “the filing date for” for “the close of” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 97–248, § 346(c)(1)(B)Subsec. (f)(3), (4). , added par. (3) and redesignated former par. (3) as (4).Pub. L. 97–248, § 346(c)(1)(C)Subsec. (g). , substituted “the filing date (as defined in subsection (f)(3)) for the taxable year” for “the close of the taxable year”.Pub. L. 97–248, § 346(c)(1)(D)Subsecs. (i), (j). , added subsec. (i) and redesignated former subsec. (i) as (j).Pub. L. 97–248, § 346(b)1980—Subsecs. (h), (i). added subsec. (h) and redesignated former subsec. (h) as (i).Pub. L. 96–2231978—Subsec. (f)(2). , substituted in heading “Certain credit carrybacks” for “Investment credit carryback”, designated existing provision as subpar. (A), and in subpar. (A) as so designated inserted heading “In general” and extended the application of provision to credit carrybacks, previously limited to investment credit carrybacks, included other credit carrybacks, and added subpar. (B).Pub. L. 95–628, § 8(c)(3)(A)Subsec. (f)(4), (5). , struck out pars. (4) and (5) which provided for work incentive program credit carrybacks and new employee credit carrybacks, respectively.Pub. L. 95–628, § 8(c)(3)(B)1977—Subsec. (f)(5). added par. (5).Pub. L. 95–301976—Subsec. (b). , struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1906(b)(13)(A)Subsec. (f)(4). , inserted “, an investment credit carryback,” after “net operating loss carryback”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 2107(g)(2)(D)Subsecs. (h), (i). , redesignated subsec. (i) as (h). Former subsec. (h), which related to a refund within 45 days after filing claim for refund of interest equalization tax paid on securities sold to foreigners, was struck out.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1904(b)(10)(A)(iv)1975—Subsec. (a). substituted “an annual rate established under section 6621” for “the rate of 6 percent per annum”.Pub. L. 93–6251973—Subsecs. (h), (i). added subsec. (f)(1). , (B), substituted “loss or capital loss carryback” for “loss carryback” in heading, and “net operating loss or net capital loss” for “net operating loss” wherever appearing in text.Pub. L. 91–172, § 512(e)(4)(A)Subsec. (f)(2). , substituted “loss carryback or a capital loss carryback” for “loss carryback”.Pub. L. 91–172, § 512(e)(4)(C)1967—Subsec. (f)(2). inserted “, or, with respect to any portion of an investment credit carryback from a taxable year attributable to a net operating loss carryback from a subsequent taxable year, such overpayment shall be deemed not to have been made prior to the close of such subsequent taxable year” after “such investment credit carryback arises”.Pub. L. 90–2251966—Subsec. (e). inserted “or, in case the return is filed after such last date, is refunded within 45 days after the date the return is filed” after “(determined without regard to any extension of time for filing the return)” and changed heading to reflect amendment.Pub. L. 89–7211964—Subsec. (f). added par. (3) and inserted “or adjustment for certain unused deductions” in heading.Pub. L. 88–5711962—Subsec. (f). designated existing provisions as par. (1) and added par. (2).Pub. L. 87–8341958—Subsec. (b)(1). , struck out “, but if the amount against which the credit is taken is an additional assessment, then to the date of the assessment of that amount” after “taken”.Pub. No Interest on Individual Income Tax Refunds for 1974 Refunded Within 60 Days After Return Is Filed, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 94–12, title I, § 101(b)89 Stat. 28Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Mar. 29, 1975Oct. 22, 1986“In applying section 6611(e) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (relating to income tax refund within 45 days after return is filed) in the case of any overpayment of tax imposed by subtitle A of such Code by an individual (other than an estate or trust and other than a nonresident alien individual) for a taxable year beginning in 1974, ‘60 days’ shall be substituted for ‘45 days’ each place it appears in such section 6611(e).”

Effective Date of 1966 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 89–721, § 1(b)80 Stat. 1150Nov. 2, 1966“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply with respect to refunds made more than 45 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Nov. 2, 1966

L. 85–866section 83(d) of Pub. L. 85–866section 6601 of this titleDec. 31, 1957

No Interest on Individual Income Tax Refunds for 1974 Refunded Within 60 Days After Return Is Filed, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 94–12, title I, § 101(b)89 Stat. 28Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Mar. 29, 1975Oct. 22, 1986“In applying section 6611(e) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (relating to income tax refund within 45 days after return is filed) in the case of any overpayment of tax imposed by subtitle A of such Code by an individual (other than an estate or trust and other than a nonresident alien individual) for a taxable year beginning in 1974, ‘60 days’ shall be substituted for ‘45 days’ each place it appears in such section 6611(e).”

children.heading: Rate
children.label: a Rate
children.text: Interest shall be allowed and paid upon any overpayment in respect of any internal revenue tax at the overpayment rate established under section 6621.

children.heading: Credits
children.label: 1 Credits
children.text: In the case of a credit, from the date of the overpayment to the due date of the amount against which the credit is taken.

children.heading: Refunds
children.label: 2 Refunds
children.text: In the case of a refund, from the date of the overpayment to a date (to be determined by the Secretary) preceding the date of the refund check by not more than 30 days, whether or not such refund check is accepted by the taxpayer after tender of such check to the taxpayer. The acceptance of such check shall be without prejudice to any right of the taxpayer to claim any additional overpayment and interest thereon.

children.heading: Late returns
children.label: 3 Late returns
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1) or (2) in the case of a return of tax which is filed after the last date prescribed for filing such return (determined with regard to extensions), no interest shall be allowed or paid for any day before the date on which the return is filed.

children.heading: Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 85–866, title I, § 83(c)72 Stat. 1664Sept. 2, 1958
children.label: c Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 85–866, title I, § 83(c)72 Stat. 1664Sept. 2, 1958

children.heading: Advance payment of tax, payment of estimated tax, and credit for income tax withholding
children.label: d Advance payment of tax, payment of estimated tax, and credit for income tax withholding
children.text: The provisions of section 6513 (except the provisions of subsection (c) thereof, applicable in determining the date of payment of tax for purposes of determining the period of limitation on credit or refund, shall be applicable in determining the date of payment for purposes of subsection (a).

children.heading: Disallowance of interest on certain overpayments
children.label: e Disallowance of interest on certain overpayments

children.heading: Refunds within 45 days after return is filed
children.label: 1 Refunds within 45 days after return is filed
children.text: If any overpayment of tax imposed by this title is refunded within 45 days after the last day prescribed for filing the return of such tax (determined without regard to any extension of time for filing the return) or, in the case of a return filed after such last date, is refunded within 45 days after the date the return is filed, no interest shall be allowed under subsection (a) on such overpayment.

children.heading: Refunds after claim for credit or refund
children.label: 2 Refunds after claim for credit or refund
A
children.text: the taxpayer files a claim for a credit or refund for any overpayment of tax imposed by this title, and

children.label: B
children.text: such overpayment is refunded within 45 days after such claim is filed,

children.heading: IRS initiated adjustments
children.label: 3 IRS initiated adjustments
children.text: If an adjustment initiated by the Secretary, results in a refund or credit of an overpayment, interest on such overpayment shall be computed by subtracting 45 days from the number of days interest would otherwise be allowed with respect to such overpayment.

children.heading: Certain withholding taxes
children.label: 4 Certain withholding taxes
children.text: In the case of any overpayment resulting from tax deducted and withheld under chapter 3 or 4, paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) shall be applied by substituting “180 days” for “45 days” each place it appears.

children.heading: Refund of income tax caused by carryback or adjustment for certain unused deductions
children.label: f Refund of income tax caused by carryback or adjustment for certain unused deductions

children.heading: Net operating loss or capital loss carryback
children.label: 1 Net operating loss or capital loss carryback
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), if any overpayment of tax imposed by subtitle A results from a carryback of a net operating loss or net capital loss, such overpayment shall be deemed not to have been made prior to the filing date for the taxable year in which such net operating loss or net capital loss arises.

children.heading: Foreign tax credit carrybacks
children.label: 2 Foreign tax credit carrybacks
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), if any overpayment of tax imposed by subtitle A results from a carryback of tax paid or accrued to foreign countries or possessions of the United States, such overpayment shall be deemed not to have been made before the filing date for the taxable year in which such taxes were in fact paid or accrued, or, with respect to any portion of such credit carryback from a taxable year attributable to a net operating loss carryback or a capital loss carryback from a subsequent taxable year, such overpayment shall be deemed not to have been made before the filing date for such subsequent taxable year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), if any overpayment of tax imposed by subtitle A results from a credit carryback, such overpayment shall be deemed not to have been made before the filing date for the taxable year in which such credit carryback arises, or, with respect to any portion of a credit carryback from a taxable year attributable to a net operating loss carryback, capital loss carryback, or other credit carryback from a subsequent taxable year, such overpayment shall be deemed not to have been made before the filing date for such subsequent taxable year.

children.heading: Credit carryback defined
children.label: B Credit carryback defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “credit carryback” has the meaning given such term by section 6511(d)(4)(C).

children.heading: Filing date
children.label: A Filing date
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “filing date” means the last date prescribed for filing the return of tax imposed by subtitle A for the taxable year (determined without regard to extensions).

children.label: I
children.text: any overpayment described in paragraph (1), (2), or (3) shall be treated as an overpayment for the loss year,

children.label: II
children.text: such subsection shall be applied with respect to such overpayment by treating the return for the loss year as not filed before claim for such overpayment is filed.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a carryback of a net operating loss or net capital loss, the taxable year in which such loss arises,

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a carryback of taxes paid or accrued to foreign countries or possessions of the United States, the taxable year in which such taxes were in fact paid or accrued (or, with respect to any portion of such carryback from a taxable year attributable to a net operating loss carryback or a capital loss carryback from a subsequent taxable year, such subsequent taxable year), and

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a credit carryback (as defined in paragraph (3)(B)), the taxable year in which such credit carryback arises (or, with respect to any portion of a credit carryback from a taxable year attributable to a net operating loss carryback, a capital loss carryback, or other credit carryback from a subsequent taxable year, such subsequent taxable year).

children.heading: Application of subparagraph (B) where section 6411(a) claim filed
children.label: C Application of subparagraph (B) where section 6411(a) claim filed
i
children.text: files a claim for refund of any overpayment described in paragraph (1), (2), or (3) with respect to the taxable year to which a loss or credit is carried back, and

children.label: ii
children.text: subsequently files an application under section 6411(a) with respect to such overpayment,

children.heading: No interest until return in processible form
children.label: g No interest until return in processible form

children.text: For purposes of subsections (b)(3) and (e), a return shall not be treated as filed until it is filed in processible form.

children.label: A
children.text: such return is filed on a permitted form, and

children.label: i
children.text: the taxpayer’s name, address, and identifying number and the required signature, and

children.label: ii
children.text: sufficient required information (whether on the return or on required attachments) to permit the mathematical verification of tax liability shown on the return.

children.heading: Prohibition of administrative review
children.label: h Prohibition of administrative review
children.text: For prohibition of administrative review, see section 6406.
